trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Sweden exports machinery, vehicles, pharmaceuticals, and telecom equipment to South Africa; South Africa exports minerals, precious metals, chemicals, and automotive parts to Sweden. Swedish firms (Volvo, Ericsson, SKF, IKEA) have established operations in South Africa.

diplomatic.summary: Sweden and South Africa maintain constructive diplomatic relations. Sweden was an opponent of apartheid and has longstanding historical ties to the ANC. However, South Africa's BRICS alignment, its abstention on Russia-Ukraine UN votes, and its ICJ case against Israel create divergence from Swedish positions. Development cooperation through Sida supports some bilateral engagement.

military.summary: No significant military ties. Both countries maintain independent defence policies. Sweden focuses on NATO. South Africa maintains a non-aligned defence posture. Some historical Swedish support for African peacekeeping through UN frameworks.

financial.summary: Swedish pension funds hold South African equities and bonds as part of emerging market exposure. Swedish firms (Volvo, Ericsson) have significant South African operations. South African mining conglomerates have some Sweden-listed equity but limited direct FDI.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Sweden assists South Africa through development aid in renewable energy transitions. Swedish firms (Vestas, ABB) participate in South Africa's renewable energy procurement programmes. No significant direct fossil fuel trade.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration, particularly in environmental science, sustainable development, and health research. Sida-funded research partnerships with South African universities. Collaboration in HIV/AIDS research and climate adaptation science.
